2026-04-07T18:29:31.6266963Z   
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6267814Z   1. The `CodeTransparencyRedirectPolicy` is an **internal** class - not public API. The change adds caching of the primary node URL for redirect optimization.
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6268877Z

2026-04-07T18:29:31.6270638Z      > Improved redirect performance for write operations by caching the latest primary node URL from redirect responses and reusing it for subsequent non-GET requests.
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6271661Z   
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6272593Z   3. The README does mention `CodeTransparencyRedirectPolicy` in the "What Changed" from beta.7 but the internal class doesn't need to be in the README since it's internal.
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6273710Z      
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6276368Z   Now I'm reviewing the README structure against the canonical format. The main sections are present—getting started, thread safety, examples, key concepts, troubleshooting, next steps, and contributing—but the ordering doesn't align with the standard pattern. Thread safety appears in an unusual position rather than following the typical flow.
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6278430Z   
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6279977Z   The performance optimization for redirect caching is an internal implementation detail that's already captured in the CHANGELOG, and since the affected class is marked internal, it doesn't need README coverage. 2026-04-07T18:29:31.6281956Z   
2026-04-07T18:29:31.6286565Z   Looking at the documentation structure, there are two minor issues worth noting: the README has "Examples" appearing before "Key Concepts" which breaks the standard documentation flow, and the "Thread safety" section is oddly positioned within "Getting started" rather than as its own section. The "Troubleshooting" section is also quite sparse, only mentioning Response objects. These are structural quirks rather than missing critical information, since the CHANGELOG already documents the bug fix and the code changes don't expose any new public APIs that would require additional documentation. The code change only affects an internal class and the CHANGELOG is already updated, so the structural issues in the README are pre-existing ... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2150 chars]

Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-07T18:29:31.8031177Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T18:28:52.140092201Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Core;\nusing Azure.Core.Pipeline;\n\nnamespace Azure.Security.CodeTransparency\n{\n    /// \u003csummary\u003e\n    /// An \u003csee cref=\"HttpPipelinePolicy\"/\u003e that follows HTTP 307 and 308 redirect responses\n    /// while preserving the Authorization header.\n    /// \u003c/summary\u003e\n    /// \u003cremarks\u003e\n    /// Code Transparency Service nodes may return 307 (Temporary Redirect) or 308 (Permanent Redirect)\n    /// responses to route write operations to the primary node. The standard redirect behavior in .NET\n    /// strips the Authorization header on cross-domain redirects for security reasons. However, CTS\n    /// redirects occur between trusted nodes within the same ledger, so the Authorization header must\n    /// be preserved for the redirected request to succeed.\n    /// \u003c/remarks\u003e\n    internal sealed class CodeTransparencyRedirectPolicy : HttpPipelinePolicy\n    {\n        private const int MaxRedirects = 5;\n        private readonly object _primaryNodeLock = new object();\n        private Uri _primaryNodeBaseUri;\n\n        /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n        public override void Process(HttpMessage message, ReadOnlyMemory\u003cHttpPipelinePolicy\u003e pipeline)\n        {\n            ProcessAsync(message, pipeline, false).EnsureCompleted();\n        }\n\n        /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n        public override ValueTask ProcessAsync(HttpMessage message, ReadOnlyMemory\u003cHttpPipelinePolicy\u003e pipeline)\n        {\n            return ProcessAsync(message, pipeline, true);\n        }\n\n        private async ValueTask ProcessAsync(HttpMessage message, ReadOnlyMemory\u003cHttpPipelinePolicy\u003e pipeline, bool async)\n        {\n            TryApplyCachedPrimaryNode(message.Request);\n\n            if (async)\n            {\n                await ProcessNextAsync(message, pipeline).ConfigureAwait(false);\n            }\n            else\n            {\n                ProcessNext(message, pipeline);\n            }\n\n            int redirectCount = 0;\n\n            while (IsRedirectResponse(message.Response.Status))\n            {\n                if (++redirectCount \u003e MaxRedirects)\n                {\n                    // Too many redirects; return the last redirect response as-is.\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                if (!message.Response.Headers.TryGetValue(\"Location\", out string location))\n                {\n                    // No Location header; return the redirect response as-is.\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                Uri redirectUri = BuildRedirectUri(message.Request.Uri.ToUri(), location);\n\n                CachePrimaryNode(redirectUri);\n\n                // Disallow redirect from HTTPS to HTTP.\n                if (string.Equals(message.Request.Uri.Scheme, \"https\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) \u0026\u0026\n                    !string.Equals(redirectUri.Scheme, \"https\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\n                {\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                // Update the request URI to the redirect target.\n                // The Authorization header is intentionally preserved because CTS redirects\n                // are between trusted nodes within the same ledger.\n                message.Request.Uri.Reset(redirectUri);\n\n                // Dispose of the redirect response before re-sending.\n                message.Response.Dispose();\n\n                if (async)\n                {\n                    await ProcessNextAsync(message, pipeline).ConfigureAwait(false);\n                }\n                else\n                {\n                    ProcessNext(message, pipeline);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static bool IsRedirectResponse(int statusCode)\n        {\n            return statusCode == 307 || statusCode == 308;\n        }\n\n        private static Uri BuildRedirectUri(Uri requestUri, string location)\n        {\n            Uri redirectUri = new Uri(location, UriKind.RelativeOrAbsolute);\n\n            if (!redirectUri.IsAbsoluteUri)\n            {\n                redirectUri = new Uri(requestUri, redirectUri);\n            }\n\n            return redirectUri;\n        }\n\n        private void TryApplyCachedPrimaryNode(Request request)\n        {\n            if (request.Method == RequestMethod.Get)\n            {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            Uri primaryNodeBaseUri = Volatile.Read(ref _primaryNodeBaseUri);\n            if (primaryNodeBaseUri == null)\n            {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            request.Uri.Reset(BuildUriWithPrimaryHost(request.Uri.ToUri(), primaryNodeBaseUri));\n        }\n\n        private void CachePrimaryNode(Uri redirectUri)\n        {\n            Uri candidatePrimary = GetPrimaryNodeBaseUri(redirectUri);\n            if (candidatePrimary == null)\n            {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            lock (_primaryNodeLock)\n            {\n                _primaryNodeBaseUri = candidatePrimary;\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static Uri GetPrimaryNodeBaseUri(Uri uri)\n        {\n            if (uri == null || !uri.IsAbsoluteUri)\n            {\n                return null;\n            }\n\n            return new UriBuilder(uri.Scheme, uri.Host, uri.IsDefaultPort ? -1 : uri.Port).Uri;\n        }\n\n        private static Uri BuildUriWithPrimaryHost(Uri requestUri, Uri primaryNodeBaseUri)\n        {\n            var builder = new UriBuilder(requestUri)\n            {\n                Scheme = primaryNodeBaseUri.Scheme,\n                Host = primaryNodeBaseUri.Host,\n                Port = primaryNodeBaseUri.IsDefaultPort ? -1 : primaryNodeBaseUri.Port\n            };\n\n            return builder.Uri;\n        }\n    }\n}\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.CodeTransparency/src/CodeTransparencyRedirectPolicy.cs"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8058944Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T18:28:52.153354985Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.9 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added `CodeTransparencyClientSettings` to support creating a `CodeTransparencyClient` from `IConfiguration`, including configuration-based credential resolution and dependency injection registration.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Improved redirect performance for write operations by caching the latest primary node URL from redirect responses and reusing it for subsequent non-GET requests. The cache is lazily populated and refreshed whenever the service redirects to a different primary node.\n\n### Other Changes\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.8 (2026-03-02)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fixes thread unsafe code in `VerifyTransparentStatement`. The code reused sha256 instances across multiple threads, which caused exceptions to be thrown when multiple threads were verifying statements at the same time. The fix was to create new sha256 instances for each verification operation instead of reusing them.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.7 (2026-02-17)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added `CodeTransparencyRedirectPolicy` to automatically follow HTTP 307/308 redirects while preserving the Authorization header. Previously, redirects between Confidential Ledger nodes could return HTTP 307/308 responses that were not automatically followed by the default pipeline, causing these requests to fail unless clients implemented redirect handling themselves.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.6 (2025-12-17)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- A new option to pass transparent statement verification key sets mapped to domain names for offline verification using `CodeTransparencyVerificationOptions.OfflineKeys`\n- A new option to restrict the use of a network resolution of the ledger keys when using `OfflineKeys` with `CodeTransparencyVerificationOptions.OfflineKeysBehavior`\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.5 (2025-10-20)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Exposed `CborUtils` to extract values from CBOR maps, which would otherwise require working directly with a CBOR library\n- Added new static verification method `CodeTransparencyClient.VerifyTransparentStatement` which accepts `CodeTransparencyVerificationOptions`, this allows verifying receipts from specific issuers \n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated samples\n- Fixed grammar in README files\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.4 (2025-05-06)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Added `virtual` keyword to method `CodeTransparencyClient.RunTransparentStatementVerification`\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.3 (2025-03-31)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Aligned with the latest changes (Feb 25) of the IETF draft: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-scitt-architecture/\n- Updated receipt verification logic.\n- Exposed `JsonModelWriteCore` for model serialization procedure.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.2 (2024-03-27)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Do not fail the submission of the entry if it responds with HTTP status 202\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2024-03-26)\n\nInitial release of the client.\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.CodeTransparency/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}

The service stores [COSE signature envelopes][COSE_RFC] in the Merkle tree and issues signed inclusion proofs as [receipts][SCITT_RECEIPT_RFC].\n\n- [OSS server application source code][Service_source_code]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nEnsure you have access to the correct NuGet feed.\n\nInstall the client library via NuGet:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Security.CodeTransparency --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- A running, accessible Signing Transparency service\n- Ability to create `COSE_Sign1` envelopes (see [example script][CTS_claim_generator_script])\n- Your signer details (CA certificate) must be configured in the running service (see [configuration options][CTS_configuration_doc])\n- Obtain a valid [REDACTED] if service authentication requires one (see [example](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.CodeTransparency/samples/Sample3_UseYourCredentials.md))\n\n### Thread safety\n\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.\n\n### Authenticate the client\n\nObtain a valid [REDACTED] if the service requires authentication (see [example](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.CodeTransparency/samples

2026-04-07T18:29:31.8414751Z       },
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8414812Z       {
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8414895Z         "name": "missing_data",
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8415470Z         "description": "Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing."
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8415542Z       },
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8415603Z       {
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8415823Z         "name": "report_incomplete",
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8417573Z         "description": "Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-07T18:29:31.8417655Z       },
